{"step_by_step_thinking": "The child is 13 months old and un-immunized. We need to follow the latest immunization schedule for this age group.

2. Pentavalent vaccine: This includes diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, hepatitis B, and Haemophilus influenzae type b. The pentavalent vaccine is commonly administered at 6–12 months and again at 18 months. A 13-month-old would typically receive the second dose of pentavalent vaccine. The option says '3 Pentavalent' which is incorrect — only 2 doses are typically given in the routine schedule. So '3 Pentavalent' is not standard.
